failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for DSCR - Investment. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, 2-4 Units
New Jersey is not eligible for 2nd liens in wholesale/retail channels
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Occupancy 'Investment Property' not eligible. Available: Primary Residence
Super Jumbo program only available for Primary (Owner Occupied) occupancy - Non-Owner Occupied not eligible
Multi-Family 5-8 Units is not eligible for Investor DSCR - refer to DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program

Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Foreign National, Investment Property, FICO Score 784. Available: Single Family, PUD, 2-4 Units, Warrantable Condo, Non-Warrantable Condo, Condotel
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Investment Property. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, Non-Warrantable Condo, Co-op, Condotel, 2-4 Units
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Investment Property. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, 2-4 Units
Property type 'Residential 5-8 Units' not eligible for Investment Property. Available: SFR, PUD, Warrantable Condo, 2-4 Units
Multi-Family 5-8 Units is not eligible for Investor DSCR No Ratio - refer to DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) program

specifications: Must be current, unexpired government-issued photo identification for all borrowers
Must include full legal name matching loan application
Acceptable forms: Driver's License, Passport, State ID, or Permanent Resident Card
Must be legible with photo, signature, and identifying information clearly visible
For entity borrowers: ID required for all signatories and guarantors

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity verification required per Patriot Act and NQMF underwriting requirements
Identity must be confirmed before credit, title, and entity documentation can be verified

specifications: Must be fully executed and signed by all borrowers
Must include correct SSN/ITIN for each borrower
Must reflect current subject property address or mailing address
Tax years requested must cover required look-back period per program
For DSCR Multi 5-8 Units: required for entity guarantor verification

reasons_needed.reason: IRS Form 4506-C required per NQMF Full Documentation guidelines for tax transcript authorization
Required to validate income representations and identify undisclosed liabilities or business interests

specifications: Articles of Organization/Incorporation with all amendments
Operating Agreement or Bylaws showing ownership percentages
Certificate of Good Standing from state of formation (within 90 days)
Resolution authorizing the transaction and identifying authorized signatories
Must confirm minimum 25% ownership for member whose credit score is used for qualifying
Foreign entity registration in New Jersey if entity formed out of state

reasons_needed.reason: DSCR guidelines require minimum 25% ownership for the member whose score is used for qualifying on entity-vested loans
Entity authorization and vesting must be verified for title and closing document preparation
Investment property loans commonly vest in LLC; entity documentation validates legal capacity to transact

document_requests.document_type: Borrower Certification as to Business Purpose
specifications: Must identify all borrowers by full legal name
Must identify the subject property address (5-8 unit multi-family in New Jersey)
Must state that the loan is for business/investment purpose only
Must affirm that the property will NOT be occupied by the borrower, any member of the borrower's LLC, or any family member
Must be fully executed and signed by all borrowers
Must be dated within the loan process timeframe
Must be consistent with occupancy certification and 1003 declarations showing non-owner occupied/investment property
DSCR Compliance Agreement form (or equivalent) may also be required per lender guidelines
reasons_needed: Per NQMF guidelines: 'Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit is required on all Business Purpose loans'
Per NQMF guidelines: 'Investment properties and all DSCR loans must be Business Purpose and processed as non-TRID transactions'
Required to confirm borrower acknowledgment that this is a commercial/investment transaction
Required to affirm property will not be occupied by borrower or family members
Establishes non-TRID transaction status for regulatory compliance
Required before closing on DSCR wholesale/retail transactions
document_requests.source_guideline: NQMF Investment Property & Business Purpose Loans / Business Purpose Forms
document_requests.conditions_if_missing: Loan cannot close as Business Purpose/non-TRID without executed Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit from all borrowers

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from all three bureaus (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must identify all borrowers on the loan with valid Social Security numbers
Must show credit scores from all reporting bureaus (minimum 2 scores required)
Must include complete tradeline details with payment history
Must show mortgage/housing payment history if available
Must include inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and alerts
Must certify results of public record searches for each city where borrower resided in last 2 years
Must be dated within allowed recency window per program requirements
reasons_needed: Credit score is required to validate program eligibility (minimum FICO requirements per program matrix)
Tradeline requirements must be verified: 3 tradelines 12+ months OR 2 tradelines 24+ months with recent activity
Credit report is needed to identify any credit events, inquiries, disputes, collections, or public records
Housing/mortgage payment history must be reviewed to confirm 0x30x12 or 0x60x12 requirements
Required per NQMF guidelines for all borrowers on all loan programs

specifications: Must cover minimum 12-month lookback period (24 months preferred)
Must show payment status for each month including any late payments (30/60/90/120 day)
Must identify borrower and property/loan account clearly
Must be from mortgage servicer or reflected on credit report
Must show current status and any modifications, forbearances, or delinquencies
Must support 0x30x12 housing history standard for standard eligibility
Must identify borrower name(s) matching loan application
Must identify servicer/lender name
Must identify loan/account number matching payoff statement
Must show property address matching subject property
Must show current unpaid principal balance
Must show monthly payment amount
Must be most recent statement (within 60 days)
Must show payment due date and payment history status
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require verification of borrower's housing history for all programs
Mortgage payment history is required to confirm no late payments in required lookback period
Late mortgage payments may affect qualification, pricing, or eligibility
Cash-out refinance requires clean mortgage payment history on subject property
24-month mortgage history with 0x30x12 can satisfy tradeline requirements per guidelines
Verifies existing mortgage obligation on subject property being refinanced
Confirms unpaid balance aligns with payoff statement for reconciliation
Payment history supports mortgage history verification for underwriting
Account number must match payoff statement to ensure correct lien payoff
Required to verify REO liability and housing expense for DSCR qualification
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Housing History, Tradeline Requirements sections

specifications: Must include executed lease agreements for all occupied units in subject property
Must show tenant name(s) and lease term for each unit
Must show monthly rent amount for each unit
Must have no less than 1 month remaining at time of closing OR provide evidence of month-to-month tenancy with continuance support
For month-to-month tenancy, must provide signed extension letter OR 2 months bank statements showing rent deposits
Must identify subject property address matching loan file
Single room occupancy or boarder leases are not permitted
reasons_needed: DSCR refinance transactions require property to be leased; vacant property is not eligible
Lease agreements verify current tenant occupancy and rental terms for DSCR calculation
Market rent comparison requires actual lease terms to determine lower of market rent vs. lease amount
Month-to-month leases require continuance evidence per guideline requirements
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF DSCR Long Term Rentals - Lease and Occupancy Requirements

specifications: Must provide evidence of rent loss insurance coverage for the subject property
Coverage must equal at least 6 months of PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, and Association dues)
Blanket policies covering the subject property are eligible
Must identify subject property address on declarations page or schedule
Policy must be in force at closing
Must provide hazard insurance coverage against fire and extended coverage perils
Required perils: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm (including named storms), hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide coverage on replacement cost basis (not actual cash value)
Must name all persons holding title as named insured
Must include standard or union mortgagee clause without contribution
Must show NQM Funding, LLC as mortgagee/loss payee
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must provide policy number, coverage amounts, deductible, effective/expiration dates
Must include notice of cancellation provision for mortgagee notification
Coverage must be sufficient to cover all 5-8 units in the property
Required for DSCR loans per program guidelines
Must equal at least 6 months of PITIA
Must identify subject property address
Must show coverage amounts, effective dates, and named insured
May be waived per DSCR Supreme or Investor DSCR Program Matrix
reasons_needed: DSCR program requires rent loss insurance to protect against income loss from vacancy or tenant default
Minimum 6 months PITIA coverage is mandatory per NQMF DSCR guidelines
Insurance coverage supports investment property income continuity
Hazard insurance required on all properties per NQMF guidelines
Multi-unit investment property requires comprehensive coverage for all units
Replacement cost coverage required - actual cash value not acceptable
Standard mortgagee clause protects lender interest in collateral
Loss payable clause ensures lender receives insurance proceeds
Rent Loss Insurance required for DSCR loans per NQMF guidelines
Protects against loss of rental income if property becomes uninhabitable
Coverage of 6 months PITIA required for investment property cash flow protection
DSCR loan program requirement for collateral protection
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF DSCR Rent Loss Insurance Requirements

specifications: Must provide most recent 2 consecutive months of complete bank statements
Must include all pages from each statement period
Must show borrower name and account holder information
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show full or masked account number
Must show beginning and ending balances for each statement period
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per guideline requirements
Must support funds to close calculation including closing costs and prepaid items
Must support reserve verification - sufficient balance for required PITIA months
If any deposit exceeds 50% of qualifying income or appears unusual, source documentation will be required
Must demonstrate sufficient verified liquid assets to cover required months of PITIA reserves
Reserve calculation must include Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, and Association dues (PITIA)
Asset accounts used for reserves must show clear ownership by borrower(s)
Investment and retirement accounts may be used for reserves with applicable discounting
Retirement account reserves do not need to be withdrawn - just verified as vested and accessible
For retirement accounts used as reserves, must verify vested status and withdrawal eligibility regardless of employment status
Must identify acceptable asset sources per program guidelines
DSCR Multi 5-8 Unit programs have specific reserve requirements per matrix - verify months required based on LTV and loan amount tiers
Must identify deposit date and exact amount requiring sourcing
Must identify and document source of funds with supporting documentation
Must show paper trail demonstrating transfer path into borrower's account
Acceptable sources include: payroll deposits, tax refunds, insurance proceeds, sale of assets, gifts (with proper gift documentation)
Must confirm funds are from acceptable sources under NQMF guidelines
If deposit is from sale of asset, provide bill of sale or settlement statement
If deposit is a transfer from another owned account, provide statements from both accounts showing transfer
reasons_needed: Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for closing costs, prepaid items, and applicable reserves
Cash-out refinance requires verification of funds for closing costs
DSCR Multi 5-8 Unit loans require reserve verification to meet program matrix requirements
Account ownership must be confirmed to ensure assets are accessible to borrower
Large deposits may require additional sourcing documentation
DSCR Multi 5-8 Unit loans require reserves based on program matrix requirements
Reserve requirements vary by LTV tier and loan amount - 70% LTV with high FICO typically requires 6-12 months PITIA
Cash-out refinance transactions have reserve requirements to demonstrate borrower financial stability
Investment property loans require reserves to cover potential vacancy or repair periods
Reserves ensure borrower can sustain property payments during income disruptions
Large deposits appearing on bank statements must be sourced to determine acceptability
Unverified or undocumented funds cannot be used for closing costs or reserves
Deposit sourcing ensures funds are not from prohibited sources (loan proceeds, undisclosed borrowing, etc.)
Clear audit trail protects both lender and borrower during underwriting review
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - ASSETS: Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, debt payoff, and applicable reserves. Assets must be dated within 90 days of the note date.

specifications: Must be FHLMC Form 71A or 71B (for loan amounts < $750,000) for Multi 5-8 Unit properties
Must include narrative report format with full interior inspection of each unit
Must identify subject property address matching loan application
Must identify property type as Residential 5-8 Units with unit count
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation
Must be dated within 365 days of Note date; recertification required if exceeds 120 days
Must include rent roll for all units
Must include Income and Expense Statement
Must include photos of subject property exterior/interior and street scene
Must include aerial photo
Must include sketch or floor plan of typical units
Must include map showing location of subject and comparables
Must include appraiser's qualifications and certifications
Must confirm property is in average or better condition (C1-C4; C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must confirm no structural deferred maintenance
Must confirm no health or safety violations
Must confirm minimum 400 square feet per unit
Must confirm each unit contains full kitchen and bathroom
Must confirm property is accessible and available for year-round use
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must include comparable sales analysis with sales comparison approach
Must disclose any adverse environmental or economic factors
Must include analysis of all sales of subject property within past 3 years
Commercial Broker Price Opinion (BPO) required based on sales approach
Alternatively, Field Review is permitted for Residential 5-8 Units when available
May substitute with second full appraisal if two appraisals provided
Must identify subject property address
Must review value reasonableness against appraised value
If BPO value is more than 10% below appraised value, BPO value is used for LTV purposes
In Pennsylvania or North Carolina, commercial evaluation product required instead of BPO
reasons_needed: FHLMC 71A/71B with narrative report required per NQMF guidelines for Multi 5-8 Unit properties
Collateral valuation required to establish property value for DSCR loan underwriting
LTV at 70% requires accurate appraised value determination for eligibility
Cash-out refinance requires current market value confirmation
Investment property requires rent roll and income/expense analysis
Property condition (C4 or better) must be verified for DSCR eligibility
Multi-unit properties require comprehensive interior inspection of all units
DSCR properties must be free of deferred maintenance per guidelines
Appraisal review process for Multi 5-8 Unit properties requires secondary valuation product
Commercial BPO is standard requirement unless two full appraisals provided
Value variance exceeding 10% triggers use of lower BPO value for LTV calculation
Field Review acceptable alternative for Residential 5-8 Units
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Appraisal Requirements Multi 5-8 Unit, Pages 92-98

specifications: Must be submitted through Uniform Collateral Data Portal (UCDP)
SSR Score of 2.5 or less preferred for appraisal review process
Must identify subject property and appraisal submission
Must show submission status and any flagged issues
reasons_needed: FNMA SSR Score is acceptable review product per appraisal review process
SSR Score of 2.5 or less supports collateral valuation
Standard appraisal submission requirement for loan file completeness
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Appraisal Review Process, Page 98

specifications: Must be current FEMA Flood Certificate
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone designation in Section B4
Must indicate if property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA - Zone A or V)
Must indicate if No NFIP Map applies (Section B5)
Must indicate determination result in Section D
Must be consistent with flood zone shown on appraisal report
reasons_needed: Flood zone determination required per NQMF guidelines for all properties
Determines whether flood insurance is required
Properties in Flood Zone A or V require flood insurance and FEMA program participation
Appraisal report must accurately reflect flood zone per flood certificate

specifications: FNMA Form 3170 or equivalent Assignment of Rents rider
Required for all Investment Properties
Must be attached to security instrument/mortgage
Must assign rental income rights to lender as additional security
Must identify subject property and all rental units
reasons_needed: Assignment of Rents required for all Investment Properties per NQMF guidelines
DSCR Multi 5-8 Unit is investment property requiring rental income assignment
Provides lender security interest in rental income stream
Standard requirement for investment property collateral documentation
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Appraisal Requirements 1-4 Unit Residential (applies to investment properties), Page 96

specifications: Must identify borrower/owner/vested parties matching loan application
Must identify subject property address and legal description
Must include 24-month chain of title history showing all transfers with dates, prices, buyer/seller names
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and title exceptions
Must show title insurer qualified to do business in New Jersey
Must show effective date within acceptable recency window
Must confirm first lien position for proposed mortgage
Must indicate final title policy will be issued after funding
Title policy must be 2006 ALTA standard form, ALTA short form, or state-approved equivalent
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership and vesting align with borrower on loan file
24-month title history required per NQMF guidelines to review for flipping activity or inconsistencies
Liens and exceptions must be reviewed to ensure first lien position without exception
Adverse credit impacting title must be identified for payoff or resolution prior to closing
Legal description must be verified against appraisal and deed
Title insurer must be duly authorized and licensed in New Jersey
document_requests.condition_trigger: Always required for all loan transactions

specifications: Must be recorded deed showing current ownership/vesting of subject property
Must identify all vested owners matching borrower(s) on loan application
Must show legal description matching title commitment and appraisal
Must show property address matching subject property
Must be most recent deed of record
reasons_needed: Vesting must be verified to confirm borrower has ownership interest in property being refinanced
Per NQMF guidelines, final title policy vesting must reflect name(s) of individual borrower(s)
Vesting deed confirms chain of title and ownership continuity
Legal description on deed must reconcile with title commitment for lien validity
document_requests.condition_trigger: Always required - confirms ownership for refinance transaction

document_requests.applies_to: Existing Mortgage Lien(s) on Subject Property
specifications: Must identify creditor/servicer name and contact information
Must identify loan/account number
Must show total payoff amount including principal balance, accrued interest, and fees
Must show good-through date (payoff valid through date)
Must include per diem interest amount for payoff calculation
Must identify subject property address or secured collateral
Must be dated within 30 days of anticipated closing
Must include wire instructions for payoff disbursement
reasons_needed: Cash-out refinance requires payoff of existing first lien to establish new first lien position
Payoff amount required to calculate funds to close and verify sufficient loan proceeds
Good-through date and per diem needed for accurate closing figures
Per NQMF guidelines, existing liens must be paid off to ensure clear first lien position
Title must confirm lien position without exception - existing mortgage must be satisfied
document_requests.condition_trigger: Required for refinance transaction - existing mortgage must be paid off
